top30_filing_volume.source: WIPO IP Statistics Data Center (https://www.wipo.int/ipstats/en/), WIPI 2024 report, 2023 filing data

sources.name: USPTO Open Data Portal — PTAB proceedings
sources.issuing_body: U.S. Patent and Trademark Office (Patent Trial and Appeal Board)

sources.name: Cooperative Patent Classification (CPC)
sources.issuing_body: European Patent Office / U.S. Patent and Trademark Office

sources.name: IP Australia — Australian Trade Mark Search
IP Australia — IP RAPID (data.gov.au bulk register)
Google Patents (worldwide aggregator)
sources.issuing_body: Google LLC (aggregator; not an issuing authority)
sources.notes:
Non-authoritative aggregator; primary use is cross-jurisdiction reach
where authoritative registers (PPUBS, EPO OPS, JPO) don't reach.

sources.name: U.S. International Trade Commission (EDIS + DataWeb + HTS)
sources.notes:
Classified registered_ip by analogy to PTAB: a tribunal adjudicating
registered patents is part of the registered-IP lifecycle.

sources.name: EPO Boards of Appeal Case Law Compendium
sources.notes:
Cadence follows the bundled-artifact rhythm (annual compendium
republication), not individual decision issuance.

sources.name: PCT Guidelines (PCT-EPO Guidelines for Examination)
sources.issuing_body: European Patent Office / World Intellectual Property Organization

sources.name: Unitary Patent Guidelines
sources.issuing_body: European Patent Office (Unitary Patent Court Division)

sources.name: Unified Patent Court — Statutes & Rules of Procedure
U.S. Court of Appeals for the Federal Circuit — Opinions
sources.issuing_body: U.S. Court of Appeals for the Federal Circuit

sources.name: CanLII — Canadian patent / trademark case law and statutes
WIPO Lex — global IP legislation & treaties
IPO India — IP statutes (Patents Act 1970, Designs Act 2000, Trade Marks Act 1999, Copyright Act 1957, Patent Rules 2003)
sources.issuing_body: Office of the Controller General of Patents, Designs and Trade Marks

sources.name: IPO India — Manual of Patent Practice & Procedure
sources.issuing_body: Office of the Controller General of Patents, Designs and Trade Marks

sources.name: DPMA Germany — IP statutes (PatG, MarkenG, GebrMG, DesignG, UrhG, GeschGehG)
Légifrance — French IP statutes (Code de la propriété intellectuelle + Code de commerce L.151 trade secrets)
sources.issuing_body: Direction de l'information légale et administrative (DILA)

sources.name: Taiwan — Trade Secrets Act (營業秘密法, official English translation)
sources.issuing_body: Ministry of Justice (法務部) — Laws & Regulations Database of the Republic of China

sources.name: TIPO Taiwan — Patents (invention)
sources.issuing_body: Intellectual Property Office, Ministry of Economic Affairs (TIPO/MOEA)
sources.notes: applclass=1; biblio-only (no claims/figures/abstracts in API)

sources.name: TIPO Taiwan — Utility Models
sources.issuing_body: Intellectual Property Office, Ministry of Economic Affairs (TIPO/MOEA)
sources.notes: applclass=2; TW Article 32 invention/UM twins surface via /PatentTwins

sources.name: TIPO Taiwan — Designs
sources.issuing_body: Intellectual Property Office, Ministry of Economic Affairs (TIPO/MOEA)
sources.notes: applclass=3; TW treats design as Patent Act category

sources.name: TIPO Taiwan — Trademarks
sources.issuing_body: Intellectual Property Office, Ministry of Economic Affairs (TIPO/MOEA)
sources.notes: Nice classification; image URLs only (no rendering in v1)

sources.name: KIPO Korea — Patents and Utility Models (KIPRIS Plus)
INPI France — National Trademarks
sources.issuing_body: Institut National de la Propriété Industrielle (INPI)
sources.notes: WIPO ST.66 v1.0. BYOK — production deployers must register a personal data.inpi.fr account. ``cookie_token`` is the closest closed-vocabulary match for INPI's session-bearer + XSRF flow.

sources.name: INPI France — National Designs
sources.issuing_body: Institut National de la Propriété Industrielle (INPI)
sources.notes: WIPO ST.86 v1.0. Scope = FR-national only; RCDs covered via EUIPO; Hague international registrations via WIPO Hague. FR patents covered by EPO OPS (INPADOC) — no FR/INPI/Patents row in this manifest by design.

sources.name: USPTO Fee Schedule — Patents
sources.notes: Scrapes the USPTO HTML schedule (uspto.gov/learning-and-resources/fees-and-payment/uspto-fee-schedule), filters out design + plant patent rows so the patent route covers utility prosecution only. 37 CFR §§ 1.16, 1.17, 1.18, 1.20.

sources.name: USPTO Fee Schedule — Trademarks
sources.notes: Post-2025-rule shape (no entity tiers; paper-filing surcharges modeled via FeeCondition(trigger=paper_filing)). 37 CFR §§ 2.6, 2.7.

sources.name: USPTO Fee Schedule — Designs
sources.notes: Design-specific rows only (filing, search, exam, issue, Design CPA). Shared procedural fees (extensions, appeals, petitions) come from US/USPTO/Fees/Patents.

sources.name: EPO Schedule of Fees — Patents
sources.notes: Sources from the EPO Schedule-of-Fees SPA's backing BFF (fees.apps.epo.org/prod/bff/api/fees). The BFF is undocumented but stable. Covers EPC renewals years 2-20 (codes 732-750) and Unitary Patent renewals (codes 033-050). Amounts in EUR.

sources.name: EUIPO EUTM Fee Schedule
sources.notes: Extracts F-xxx (EUTM) and M-xxx (Madrid) fees from the EUIPO Next.js SSR stream. EUR. 10-year renewal cycle. EUTMR Annex I.

sources.name: EUIPO REUD Fee Schedule
sources.notes: Post-Regulation 2024/2822 reform: back-loaded renewal curve at periods 1/2/3/4 (years 5/10/15/20 = €150/€250/€400/€700), combined application+publication fee, late-payment surcharges abolished.

sources.name: CNIPA Fee Schedule — Patents
sources.notes: Scrapes the CNIPA English mirror (english.cnipa.gov.cn/col/col3000). Single 2-column hierarchical table with Roman-numeral sections; the scraper expands year-banded annuities (e.g., "1-3 Years (Each Year) | 900") to per-year rows. v1 covers invention patents only; utility-model + design ship as separate routes when needed. Patent Law of the PRC + Implementing Regulations. Fee schedule set by State Council Pricing Bureau.

sources.name: CIPO Fee Schedule — Patents
sources.notes: Scrapes the CIPO patent fee page (ic.gc.ca/eic/site/cipointernet-internetopic.nsf/eng/wr00142.html). 4 unique HTML tables; small + standard entity tiers in CAD. Maintenance fees use English-band descriptions (e.g., "second, third and fourth anniversaries") which the scraper expands to per-year rows. CIPO publishes annually with a January 1 effective date; Patent Rules SOR/2019-251 Schedule of Fees.

sources.name: DPMA Fee Schedule — Patents
sources.notes: Scraped from the DPMA cost-info PDF (form A 9510.1) because the English HTML fee page only lists years 3-6 of the annual fee schedule and punts to the PDF for years 7-20. pypdf + regex extraction of the uniform "<6-digit code> <description> ... <amount>" row format. Fee numbers in the 31x range are patents. Statutory basis: PatKostG (Patentkostengesetz).

sources.name: KIPO Fee Schedule — Patents
sources.notes: Scrapes the KIPO English IP-system fees page (HtmlApp c=92004). KRW. 2-column Description | Fee table with section headers (Application/Examination/Annual/Others). Annuity bands 1-3, 4-6, 7-9, 10-12, 13-15, 16-25 years expanded to per-year rows. Per-claim surcharges appear as bare-amount rows after each "a. Basic fee" row and are paired by the scraper. Effective 2023-08-01 per KIPO Enforcement Rule amendment.

sources.name: IP Australia Fee Schedule — Patents
sources.notes: Scrapes IP Australia's timeframes-and-fees page (13 small Action | Fee tables). AUD. Fees revised 2024-10-01 per the four-yearly fee review; PCT-fee equivalents updated 2026-01-01. v1 GAP: annual renewal fees (years 5-20) live in Schedule 7 of the Patents Regulations 1991 and are not on this page — follow-up scraper needed to pull them from the Federal Register of Legislation.

sources.name: UKIPO Fee Schedule — Patents
sources.notes: UKIPO publishes one gov.uk publication page per patent form (~30 forms). The scraper reads the index detail page (form number/title/sub-URL), then fans out to fetch each sub-page (bounded concurrency 5, hishel cache 7d) and extracts the £-amount from the "Cost" section. Effective 2026-04-01 — first major UKIPO fee rise in years (~25% average). v1 GAP: renewal fees are published as a range (£90-£810); per-year schedule is in The Patents (Fees) Rules 2007 statutory instrument and needs a follow-up scraper against legislation.gov.uk.

sources.name: UKIPO Fee Schedule — Trademarks
sources.notes: Scrapes the gov.uk trade-mark-forms-and-fees detail page; 17 inline Form | Title | Cost tables. Renewals tagged year=10 (UKIPO TM 10-year cycle). Effective 2026-04-01.
